paper_title: Independence number in triangle-free graphs avoiding a minor
summary: The conjecture that $MR_t(s,k) \leq t^{1-\varepsilon}k$ for $s \geq 4$ and sufficiently large $t$ (with tightness at $\varepsilon = 1/s$) remains open. The source paper (arXiv:1907.12999) establishes the $s=3$ case (triangle-free) and appeared in Proceedings of the AMS in 2025, but no follow-up resolving or substantially advancing the $s \geq 4$ case was found in the indexed literature. The internal candidate reference (arXiv:2206.13635) addresses hypergraph coloring with excluded minors and does not engage with $MR_t(s,k)$ or independence numbers in $K_s$-free graphs.

internal_refs_verified.note: Abstract is about hypergraph colorings with excluded $K_t$-minors (a Hadwiger-type result by Steiner); no mention of Dvořák-Yepremyan, $MR_t(s,k)$, or independence numbers in $K_s$-free graphs. Not directly relevant to this conjecture.

notes: No follow-up resolving the $s \geq 4$ case was found after 5+ web queries. The paper was published in Proc. Amer. Math. Soc. (2025) — an AMS proceedings URL was found but returned HTTP 403. Confidence is medium rather than high because the conjecture dates to 2019 (nearly 7 years old), making the absence of a follow-up in this search less conclusive than for a very recent conjecture. The Semantic Scholar API indicated ~4 citing papers but their titles/content could not be fully retrieved within the call budget.
